name: 2% BHA Liquid Exfoliant
hero: The reference 2% salicylic acid leave-on. Correctly buffered to pH 3.2-4.0, lightweight, fragrance-free, with a 25-year reformulation history. Every other BHA is judged against this one.

scoreBreakdown.n: 2% at pH 3.2-4.0 is the sweet spot for activity vs irritation.
Light liquid; no greasy film. Layers cleanly.
Comedone reduction at 8-12 weeks replicated across studies.
₹ 3,200 / 118 mL — premium; The Inkey List BHA is half the price.

ingredients.note: Reaches inside the pore; reduces comedones.

useCases.b: Daily PM, alternate with retinoid until tolerated, then layer.
Body application: arms, thighs. Most effective ingredient short of Rx.
Reduces sebum film; pores read smaller within 4-6 weeks.

alts.name: Beta Hydroxy Acid 2%
alts.note: Half the price; slightly thicker vehicle.

alts.name: BHA Blackhead Power Liquid
alts.note: Betaine salicylate; slightly weaker than true SA.

faq.a: Most skin tolerates daily after a 2-week ramp. Start every other night, increase as tolerated. Daily is the goal — sporadic use gives sporadic results.
On most adult skin, no — buffer with a moisturiser between. On sensitised skin, alternate nights instead.
Most dermatology guidelines advise stopping leave-on SA in pregnancy. Switch to azelaic acid or glycolic acid as a milder alternate.

faq.q: Is it really worth 2× The Inkey List?
faq.a: Marginally — better vehicle, smoother finish, more research bandwidth behind the brand. The Inkey delivers 90% of the benefit at 50% of the price. Either is a reasonable choice.

sources.n: Kornhauser A et al. Salicylic acid in acne. Cosm Toiletries 2011.
Webster GF. Topical therapy for acne. JAAD 1995.
Lebwohl M et al. Salicylic acid pH-activity relationship. JID 2007.
